---
title: "Ross Wardrup"
subtitle: "Spatial epidemiology · public-health GIS · applied statistical software"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

I work at the point where public-health data, geography, and software meet. Most
of what I do falls into two categories: building the tools and pipelines that
turn messy surveillance and environmental data into something you can actually
analyze, and doing the analysis itself — spatial pattern detection, hierarchical
and spatiotemporal models, and the validation work that keeps the conclusions
honest.

I am an MPH Epidemiology student at the University of Memphis. My thesis is a
quantitative study of West Nile virus mosquito surveillance in California —
how environmental conditions, surveillance effort, and space and time relate to
where the virus shows up in mosquito pools. Alongside the coursework I write
software for a living: I develop GIS and risk-modeling tools in support of
FEMA's Hazus program, and I build open tools for spatial epidemiology in
low-resource settings.

The through-line is methods. I care about getting the spatial statistics right,
about surveillance and detection bias being treated as part of the problem
rather than ignored, and about analyses that someone else can reproduce.

## What I'm working on

- **A West Nile virus thesis** on California mosquito surveillance — infection
  rate in mosquito pools as a function of environment, effort, and space-time
  structure, using Bayesian hierarchical spatiotemporal models.
  [Read more →](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd)

- **MESA**, a C++ desktop application for public-health mapping in sparse,
  rural areas — geocoding and spatial cluster detection with the statistics
  validated against established libraries.
  [Read more →](projects/mesa.qmd)

- **GIS and risk-modeling software** in support of FEMA's Hazus program —
  hazard and loss workflows, spatial data engineering, and validation.
  [Read more →](projects/hazus.qmd)

- **Applied public-health GIS** with a local health department — respiratory
  illness hot-spot analysis and immunization-compliance reporting.
  [Read more →](projects/seuhd-practicum.qmd)

## Elsewhere

- Code: [github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond)
- Email: [minorsecond@gmail.com](mailto:minorsecond@gmail.com)
- [Curriculum vitae](cv.qmd)
